decisions.rationale: calibrated confidence ('largely' vs 'essentially') strengthens credibility with a skeptic and costs nothing in voice
completing the chain into the reliability section tightens the argument and stays in the writer's cadence
decisions.applied_text: margin migrates up the stack — toward whoever turns a raw model into a dependable workflow.

decisions.rationale: the intent (tie 'trustworthy' to the buyer's job) is right, but the reviewer's full clause is wordy; apply the intent in fewer words to keep the writer's punchy rhythm
decisions.applied_text: It is the system that makes an ordinary model trustworthy enough to hand it a real workflow.

decisions.rationale: the brief explicitly asks for 'a little contrarian' with opinions welcome; 'Underwrite the repricing, not the apocalypse' is exactly that voice — flattening it to neutral phrasing would obey the note while erasing the POV. Suggest, don't replace.
decisions.applied_text: Underwrite the repricing, not the apocalypse.

voice_notes: PROTECT: the staccato declaratives — 'That is not a demo. That is a P&L line.' and 'Underwrite the repricing, not the apocalypse.'
PROTECT: first-person contrarian asides ('I want to pressure-test the thesis rather than repeat it')
PROTECT: the sharp one-liners ('a bad place to be the fifth entrant')
the goal is persuade-an-investor; keep the confident, mildly contrarian register the brief set — do not sand it into a neutral research note
source: content-research-writer step 6 — Preserve Writer's Voice (suggest don't replace; one edit REJECTED to protect POV; tone matched to the brief's style target)
